topic.what: Predicate blind signature protocol enabling users to certify TLS data with standard RSA-PSS signatures
topic.how: Extends DECO architecture replacing ZK proof verification with predicate blind signatures from notary, allowing off-the-shelf signature verification
topic.why_matters: Enables verifier-side transparency using only standardized cryptography, removing need for non-standardized ZK tools in real-world deployments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to generate NIZK proof for 26KB PDF
characteristics.context: Initial implementation with full circuit; chunked approach scales linearly

characteristics.what_it_measures: RAM required during NIZK proof generation
characteristics.context: For 26KB PDF; chunked approach reduces to constant per-chunk memory

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to verify the generated proof
characteristics.context: Groth16 proofs 800 bytes, verified with standard tools
Tradeoff: SNARK succinct vs VitH low-memory

characteristics.what_it_measures: Data processing rate in chunked mode
Adaptivity of security against corrupted signers
characteristics.context: Chose multisig over threshold to achieve adaptive security

novelty: Predicate blind RSA-PSS signatures producing IETF-standard outputs
Blind multisignature scheme enabling adaptive security against corrupted notaries
Practical integration with DECO replacing ZK proofs at verifier side
Chunked proof generation achieving constant memory for arbitrary input sizes
open_problems: Formal security proof for full ACTS architectural integration
Usability improvements for decentralized notary setting
Integration with W3C Verifiable Credentials standard
Handling larger PDF files without excessive resource requirements
